title: Growing Annuity: Joey, Connor, and Grant are thinking about selling their podcast's...
summary: Calculate the effective annual rate or convert between APR and EAR under different compounding assumptions.
prompt:
Joey, Connor, and Grant are thinking about selling their podcast's media rights to
Fuji TV フジテレビ). The Japanese television network offered a particular deal,
and it goes as follows. They're going to be paid $99.97 in 3 year(s) from now, and
these initial cash flows will grow by 28.10% per year for 2 years. However, the
growth rate will fall to a fixed 6.95% thereafter. Note that Joey, Connor, and
Grant's required rate of return is 14.07%. What is the present value of this deal?
And, should they accept this deal if they were offered a $430 lump sum payment
by a competing Japanese television network?
Select the closest answer$1,186.83$1,278.21$1,342.12$1,374.07$1,207.91

Step 1 Highlight Relevant Information & Define Key Variables
Calculator studio solutions 4
Joey, Connor, and Grant are thinking about selling their podcast's media rights to
Fuji TV フジテレビ). The Japanese television network offered a particular deal,
and it goes as follows. They're going to be paid $99.97 in 3 year(s) from now,
and these initial cash flows will grow by 28.10% per year for 2 years. However,
the growth rate will fall to a fixed 6.95% thereafter. Note that Joey, Connor, and
Grant's required rate of return is 14.07%. What is the present value of this deal?
And, should they accept this deal if they were offered a $430 lump sum payment
by a competing Japanese television network?
PMT $99.97
K 14.07%
G35 28.10%
G5∞ 6.95%
Note that this set of cash flows will begin 3 year(s) from now; therefore, it is
crucial that you take this into account when building your timeline in later steps.
Also, it is best practice to use our TVM flowchart to uncover which formula(s) we
should use in this scenario.
Once we've highlighted all the relevant information available and defined key
variables, it is evident that:
Payments are occuring for a defined period between year 3 and year 5 for which
we can assume that they're happening at the end of the period (i.e., year).
However, these payments are growing at a rate of 28.10% per year during that
Calculator studio solutions 5
time. Therefore, we must use the Growing Annuity formula for this section of the
problem.
Payments are occuring for an undefined period of time thereafter (i.e., after year
5. In this case, it doesn't matter whether payments are set at the beginning or
end of the period. Then, these payments are growing at a rate of 6.95% per year
forever. Therefore, we must use the Growing Perpetuity formula for this section
of the problem.
Step 2 Building our Timelines
Timeline
A few things to note:
PMT G1 stands for the Growing Annuity cash flows
PMT G2 stands for the Growing Perpetuity cash flows
Why don't we break down this complex timeline into two sections? One for the
Growing Annuity section of this problem, and the other for the Growing Perpetuity
section. It's always recommended to break-down problems into bite-sized pieces
such that it becomes easier to solve the exercise successfully.
Growing Annuity timeline
Growing Perpetuity timeline
